content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://unfccc.int/sites/default/files/resource/Global_Carbon_Budgets_Current_and_Future_Permafrost_Emissions_as_Large_as_Major_Emitters_SusanaHancock.pdf", "source_title": "[PDF] Current and Future Permafrost Emissions as Large as Major Emitters", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Increasing climate-driven disturbances have weakened or reversed the long-term CO 2 sink in Boreal forest and Tundra biomes with inland wate...", "direct_quote": "Increasing climate-driven disturbances have weakened or reversed the long-term CO 2 sink in Boreal forest and Tundra biomes with inland waters and wetlands contributing high methane fluxes.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Today, at about 1.2\u00b0C, we are already committed to losing about 25% of surface permafrost.", "direct_quote": "Today, at about 1.2\u00b0C, we are already committed to losing about 25% of surface permafrost.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "As temperatures have risen, especially since about 1950, permafrost has not only declined in area but thawed to deeper depth and greater vol...", "direct_quote": "As temperatures have risen, especially since about 1950, permafrost has not only declined in area but thawed to deeper depth and greater volume, beginning to release its stored carbon.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
